Purpose: Pods in 1Az are deleted to test service resiliency to az down scenario
Comment:
Service health should degrade as enqueue/dequeue calls for the broker start failing with error rate going up, and total rates coming down.

As messages may be routed to another instance, broker and DB usage may go up, with rate going back up.

Finally kubernetes should spin up another pod, DB and broker usage, along with rate should go back to normal levels.

Purpose: QPid container is killed gracefully with a SIGTERM signal
Comment:
Service health should degrade as enqueue/dequeue calls for the broker start failing with error rate going up, and total rates coming down.

As messages may be routed to another instance, broker and DB usage may go up, with rate going back up.

Finally kubernetes should spin up another pod, DB and broker usage, along with rate should go back to normal levels.

Purpose: QPid container is killed ungracefully with a SIGKILL signal
Comment:
Service health should degrade as enqueue/dequeue calls for the broker start failing with error rate going up, and total rates coming down.

As messages may be routed to another instance, broker and DB usage may go up, with rate going back up.

Finally kubernetes should spin up another pod, DB and broker usage, along with rate should go back to normal levels.

Purpose: Istio sidecar is killed gracefully with a SIGTERM signal
Istio sidecar is killed ungracefully with a SIGKILL signal
Adds significant delay for outgoing packets to simulate minor network degradation
Comment:
There should be no impact of minor network delays on service health.

There is no auto-recovery for this

Purpose: Adds significant delay for outgoing packets to simulate severe network degradation
Comment:
We should observe a degradation in Longest Waiting Time.

There is no auto-recovery for this

Purpose: Generate high CPU load on QPid container
Comment:
Performance degradation is expected, including increased request duration and reduced throughput. 

There is no auto-recovery for this

Purpose: Restart all QPid pods in a staggered sequence
Comment:
The service should maintain high availability with a brief, minor transient impact on health metrics (e.g., connection drops) as each pod restarts.

Recovery not applicable here

Purpose: Block partial/all outgoing network traffic from the QPid container
Comment: QPid will be unable to communicate with external dependencies (e.g., DB), leading to immediate operation failures. Kubernetes should eventually restart the container/pod, and service health should improve.

Purpose: Generate high memory load on QPid container
Comment:
The container may experience performance degradation or be terminated by the operating system (OOM kill).

If terminated, Kubernetes will restart the container, and service health should improve. If not terminated, there is auto-recovery and performance will remain degraded.

Purpose: Fill the local disk space on the QPid container/pod
Comment: Operations that require disk writes (e.g., logging) will fail, leading to service degradation. Kubernetes should eventually restart the container/pod, and service health should improve.

Purpose: Induce a fault that causes the QPid pod to repeatedly crash and restart
Comment:
Service health will be severely impacted for a prolonged period, showing degraded availability and high error rates until the underlying issue is resolved.

No automated recovery

Purpose: Replica count for QPid would be reduced by 1
Comment:
The service should remain operational but with reduced capacity and resilience to subsequent faults. Health metrics (e.g., CPU/Memory) on remaining pods will increase.

No automated recovery

Purpose: Throttle the outgoing network throughput of the QPid container
Comment:
Service will experience increased message backlogs and request duration due to reduced ability to send data, but will remain available.

No automated recovery
